body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:error-export
route: driver-gap

## Scenario

token-quota-exhausted: a token/quota budget is exhausted mid-turn (provider 429 / cost cap / context-window overflow); the model call fails and the turn aborts. User-observable signal per the scenario `verify`: min_turns≥1, final_state `ready`, has_waiting_state false — i.e. the session settles to `ready` after the quota failure and does NOT stick in `working`, with no `waiting` transition.

## agent_supports: yes

opencode 1.14.50 enforces and surfaces a provider/quota failure: a 429 / cost cap / context-window overflow aborts the turn and opencode records the failure on the parent assistant `message` row as `message.data.error` (APIError / UnknownError, e.g. the context-overflow `n_keep >= n_ctx` UnknownError). The quota class is one specialization of an error-aborted turn — the same surface as the opencode/turn-aborted-by-error sibling cell.

## daemon_capability: full (flipped from incapable by #493)

This verdict CHANGED since the prior assessment. The old artifact recorded `incapable` because opencode never writes a `step-finish` part with reason=error on a quota/error abort, and the watcher's terminal-turn decision read only `isTerminalPart(partData)` — so the working→ready settle was invisible.

The daemon now consumes `message.data.error` as a terminal-turn signal. `scanParts` JOINs `m.data as message_data` (watcher.go:483) and the terminal decision is `isTerminalPart(partData) || isErrorMessage(msgData)` (watcher.go:528). `isErrorMessage` (watcher.go:441-465) returns true for a non-empty `error` string or object on the message row, setting `EventActivity.Terminal = true`, which settles the turn working→ready. This is the #493 fix and is unit-tested (`TestScanSessions_ErrorMessageTerminal`, `isErrorMessage` table test, watcher_test.go:621/632). Given a recording + a working driver, the live daemon CAN observe the scenario's user-observable signal. Hence `full`.

(Narrow remaining gap, a caveat not a downgrade: a turn that errors before emitting ANY part has no part↔message join row to carry msgData, so the message error is unreachable — watcher.go:521-527. The quota path always emits at least the user `text` part, so this edge does not affect this cell.)

## driver_capability: gap:error-export

The blocker is now purely at the recording harness. The daemon reads the error from the LIVE SQLite store, but the committed REPLAY fixture is parts-only and drops it. Both export paths in drive-opencode-interactive.sh — headless (lines 411-427) and the new live-TUI tmux path (lines 250-266) — emit `json_set(p.data, '$._role', …, '$._cwd', …, '$._ts', …, '$._model', …)`: they serialize the `part.data` blob enriched with four message-derived SCALARS only. `message.data.error` is never carried into the transcript JSONL. So replay sees the user `text` part and the assistant text/tool parts but no terminal-error signal, and cannot reproduce the working→ready settle the live daemon achieves. The missing driver primitive is `error-export` — fold `message.data.error` into the exported transcript (e.g. a synthetic `_error` field, or an injected step-finish reason=error part) so the parser/watcher path the live daemon exercises can fire on replay.

The recently-added live-TUI path (`slash` / `reset_session` primitives, run_live ~lines 99-294, route guard 355-357) does NOT change this verdict. It adds a different INPUT mechanism (tmux send-keys for in-REPL slash commands) for scenarios like session-reset; its transcript EXPORT is identical parts-only SQL (lines 250-266) and still omits `message.data.error`. token-quota-exhausted needs no slash/reset step anyway — a single context-overflow `send` elicits the error on the headless path — so it would not even route to run_live. Either way the export gap stands.

## Why driver-gap, not incapable and not applicable:false

The prior artifact concluded `applicable:false` / `incapable` (observability gap). That conclusion is now stale: #493 made the daemon capable. This is queued tooling work (extend the driver's export with the `error-export` primitive), not an architectural freeze. Per the routing table, daemon=full + driver=gap:* → route `driver-gap`: extend the driver, do NOT freeze the cell. Tracked #493 (same fix unblocks the turn-aborted-by-error sibling, which carries the identical verdict).

## Unblocks when

The driver's transcript export includes `message.data.error` (the `error-export` primitive). Once present, the existing daemon path (`isErrorMessage` → Terminal=true → working→ready) resolves on replay and the cell becomes recordable via a context-overflow prompt through the existing send-only headless path.
caveats: Mocked an OpenAI insufficient_quota 429 (tools/agent-onboarding/recording/mock-openai-429) to test the quota path. FINDING: opencode RETRIES a 429 indefinitely (AI-SDK exponential backoff — 24+ requests over 180s, the turn never completes, and message.data.error is NEVER written). So opencode does not surface quota exhaustion as a terminal state — the session hangs in `working` until the process is killed; there is no working->ready settle to observe. Not recordable as a clean quota-abort. (Context-overflow IS terminal for opencode — see turn-aborted-by-error via reason=length — but that is a different scenario.) Records only if opencode changes 429 handling to abort terminally, or against a provider error class opencode treats as non-retryable.

Daemon verdict flipped incapable→full this cycle: #493 added watcher.go isErrorMessage(), which reads message.data.error as a terminal-turn signal (watcher.go:528, unit-tested TestScanSessions_ErrorMessageTerminal). The prior assessment's `incapable` was correct before that fix and is now stale.

The blocker is now driver-only (gap:error-export): the live daemon reads message.data.error from the SQLite store, but the driver's parts-only transcript export (drive-opencode-interactive.sh, both headless lines 411-427 and live-TUI lines 250-266) drops it, so a replay fixture can't reproduce the working→ready settle. Tracked #493.

The new live-TUI tmux path (slash / reset_session primitives, run_live) does not close this gap — it is a different INPUT mechanism with the SAME parts-only EXPORT; and this cell would route to the headless path regardless (no slash/reset step needed).

Narrow daemon edge (caveat, not a downgrade): a turn that errors before emitting ANY part has no part↔message join row to carry the message error (watcher.go:521-527), so it stays invisible. The quota path always emits the user text part first, so this edge does not affect this cell.

Probed on opencode 1.14.50 with the default local model lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored (LM Studio). Live store audit of ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db: step-finish reasons = stop(102)/tool-calls(165)/error(0); message.data.error rows = APIError(10)/MessageAbortedError(26)/UnknownError(3), incl. context-overflow n_keep>=n_ctx — quota/error turns persist at message level, never as a step-finish part.

sources.note: isErrorMessage (441-465) treats a non-empty message.data.error (string or object) as a terminal signal; scanParts JOINs m.data as message_data (483) and the terminal decision is isTerminalPart(partData) || isErrorMessage(msgData) (528), settling an errored/quota turn working→ready. This is the #493 daemon fix — daemon_capability is now full.

TestScanSessions_ErrorMessageTerminal (632) asserts EventActivity.Terminal=true for a turn whose only terminal signal is message.data.error; the isErrorMessage table test (621) covers string/object/nil shapes. Confirms the daemon path is live and tested, not aspirational.

Both transcript exports — headless (411-427) and live-TUI run_live (250-266) — SELECT json_set(p.data, '$._role',…, '$._cwd',…, '$._ts',…, '$._model',…): part data plus four scalars, no message.data.error. The live-TUI path (route guard 355-357) is a slash/reset INPUT mechanism with the same parts-only EXPORT. This is the gap:error-export primitive (#493): the replay fixture cannot carry the terminal-error signal the live daemon reads.

parseStepFinish maps reason=error → turn_done (164-166), but that branch is dead for opencode quota/error turns because opencode never persists a step-finish reason=error part; the terminal signal lives on message.data.error, which the watcher (not the parser) consumes via isErrorMessage.
